title: cytisus × praecox
intro: Cytisus × praecox, also known as Scotch broom, is a hybrid plant derived from the cross between Cytisus scoparius and Cytisus purpureus. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cytisus × praecox include its potential to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be limited due to concerns over toxicity and potential side effects.
